output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    TestObserver observer(observerName);
    TestObserver target(targetName);
    
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(&observer, &target);
    
    target.valueChanged(true);
    
    bool notified = observer.wasChanged();
    int callCount = observer.getCallCount();
    
    return {notified, callCount};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    auto var = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(initialValue);
    var->atomic_add(addValue);
    return var->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**
1. **Reactive Variable Creation**: Uses `std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>` to create a reactive integer variable with the given `initialValue`.
2. **Atomic Addition**: Calls `atomic_add` on the reactive variable to safely add `addValue` (handling concurrency if needed).
3. **Value Retrieval**: Returns the updated value using the `get()` method, which reflects the result of the atomic operation.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_calculation(int a, int b) {
    reaction::ReactiveVar<int> var_a;
    var_a.set(a);
    
    reaction::ReactiveVar<int> var_b;
    var_b.set(b);
    
    auto calc = reaction::ReactiveCalc<int>([&]() {
        return (var_a.get() + var_b.get()) * 2;
    });
    
    return calc.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/core/types.h>
#include <memory>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Create batch ID
    auto batch_id = BatchId::generate();
    
    // Register nodes to batch
    graph->registerToBatch(batch_id, {node1, node2});
    
    // Verify nodes are in batch
    for (const auto& node : {node1, node2}) {
        if (!graph->isNodeInBatch(node, batch_id)) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    
    // Unregister batch
    graph->unregisterBatch(batch_id);
    
    // Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    for (const auto& node : {node1, node2}) {
        if (graph->isNodeInBatch(node, batch_id)) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    TestObserverReset observer(observerName);
    TestObserverReset target(targetName);

    // Add observer to target
    target.addObserver(&observer);

    // Notify target once
    target.valueChanged(true);

    // Check observer was notified
    if (observer.getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Record initial call count
    int initialCount = observer.getCallCount();

    // Reset observer
    observer.reset();

    // Notify target again
    target.valueChanged(true);

    // Check observer wasn't notified after reset
    if (observer.getCallCount() != initialCount) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    TestObserverClosure observer(nodeName);
    TestObserverClosure subject("subject");

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(&observer, &subject);

    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    subject.valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer.wasChanged() || observer.getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset observer state
    observer.valueChanged(false);

    // Close observer node
    observer.close();

    // Notify target again
    subject.valueChanged(true);

    // Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer.wasChanged() || observer.getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(observer);
        reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    target->notify();
    
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (const auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return {totalCalls, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;

    }

#pragma once

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test reactive expression using expr API
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

3. **Evaluation**: Calling `.get()` on the expression evaluates it and returns the current result based on the reactive variables' values.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age) 
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}
    
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }
    int getAge() const { return m_age; }
    
    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name = name; }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age = age; }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    return "Name: " + person.getName() + ", Age: " + std::to_string(person.getAge());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,

                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    auto varA = reaction::Var(valueA).setName("varA");
    auto varB = reaction::Var(valueB).setName("varB");
    auto calcSum = reaction::Calc([varA, varB]() { return varA.get() + varB.get(); }).setName("calcSum");
    auto actionIncrement = reaction::Action([&varA]() { varA.set(varA.get() + 1); }).setName("actionIncrement");
    
    return varA.getName() + " " + varB.getName() + " " + calcSum.getName() + " " + actionIncrement.getName();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.reasoning:
<think>Okay, I need to implement the test_filter_trigger function using the reaction library. Let me start by understanding what each part of the problem requires.

First, the function needs to create a reactive variable. In reaction, reactive variables are created using var(). So I'll create a sensor variable initialized with initialValue.

Next, track recomputation count and last processed value. For the recomputation count, maybe I can use a reactive variable that gets incremented each time the calculation is triggered. The last processed value would just be another reactive variable that stores the value after filtering.

Then, create a filter-triggered calculation. The reaction library has a calc() function that can take dependencies and a filter. The filter here is probably based on the difference between consecutive values. So the calculation should only recompute when the difference between the new value and the previous processed value exceeds the diffThreshold.

Wait, but how does the filter work in reaction? The filter is a function that returns true when the calculation should be triggered. So the filter function would check if the absolute difference between the current sensor value and the last processed value is greater than diffThreshold. But wait, the last processed value might not be available immediately. Hmm, maybe the filter checks the difference between the current value and the previous value in the dependency chain.

Alternatively, perhaps the filter is applied to the sensor variable itself. Let me think. The reaction library's calc() can have a filter that determines whether the calculation should be invalidated and recomputed. So the filter could be a lambda that returns true when the change in the sensor value is above the threshold.

Wait, the problem says "filter-triggered calculation", so the calculation is only recomputed when the filter condition is met. So the filter is part of the calc() function.

So the steps would be:

1. Create the sensor variable with initialValue.
2. Create a reactive variable for recomputeCount, initialized to 0.

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <cmath>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::var(0);
    auto lastProcessedValue = reaction::var(initialValue);

    auto calc = reaction::calc(
        [&]() {
            lastProcessedValue.set(sensor.get());
            recomputeCount.increment();
        },
        [diffThreshold](double sensorVal, double lastProcessed) {
            return std::abs(sensorVal - lastProcessed) > diffThreshold;
        }
    );

    // Update sensor with first value
    sensor.set(firstUpdate);

    // Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount.get();

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensor.set(secondUpdate);

    // Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount.get();

    // Determine final status
    std::string status = lastProcessedValue.get() > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";

    return {status, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        if (changed) {
            m_notificationCount++;
        }
    }

    }

};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:

        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);
    
    ProfileFields fields(initialScore, false);
    
    fieldGraph.bindField(&ownerNode, fields.score());
    fieldGraph.bindField(&ownerNode, fields.active());
    
    fields.score().set(initialScore + scoreDelta);
    int afterScoreUpdate = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    fields.active().set(true);
    int afterStatusUpdate = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    return {afterScoreUpdate, afterStatusUpdate};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

    graph.registerNode(sensor1);
    graph.registerNode(sensor2);

    graph.addObserver(actuator1, sensor1);

    int notificationsAfterRollback = sensor1->notificationCount();
    
    return {notificationsAfterSecondBinding, notificationsAfterRollback};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

2. **Initial Dependency Setup**: `sensor1` is initially set to observe `actuator1`.
3. **State Preservation**: The current observer state is saved before modifying dependencies.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
private:
    std::string m_name;
    
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    const std::string& name() const { return m_name; }
};

struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    reaction::ObserverGraph& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Create observer nodes
    NamedObserverNode source("source");
    NamedObserverNode direct("direct");
    NamedObserverNode transitive("transitive");
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.registerNode(source);
    graph.registerNode(direct);
    graph.registerNode(transitive);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship: source observes direct
    graph.addObserver(source, direct);
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship: direct observes transitive
    graph.addObserver(direct, transitive);
    
    // Collect all observers reachable from the source node
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.collectObservers(source, observers);
    
    // Check if direct observer is in the collected set
    bool containsDirect = observers.contains(direct);
    
    // Check if transitive observer is in the collected set
    bool containsTransitive = observers.contains(transitive);
    
    // Return summary of the collection
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        observers.size(),
        containsDirect,
        containsTransitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::concurrency::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.registerThread(); // Register the main thread
    
    // Get thread count and safety status before creating worker thread
    auto threadCountBefore = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Create and start worker thread
    std::thread worker([]() {
        // Worker thread does minimal work and implicitly registers itself
        // via the thread manager's automatic detection
    });
    
    // Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // Get thread count and safety status after worker thread completes
    auto threadCountAfter = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        threadCountBefore,
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**
1. **Thread Manager Instance**: `ThreadManager::getInstance()` retrieves the singleton instance.
2. **Reset State**: `reset()` clears any previous state.
3. **Register Main Thread**: `registerThread()` ensures the main thread is tracked.
4. **Pre-Worker State**: Capture thread count and safety status before creating the worker thread.
5. **Worker Thread**: Created with a lambda that performs minimal work (implicitly registered via automatic detection).
6. **Join Worker Thread**: Ensures the main thread waits for the worker to complete.
7. **Post-Worker State**: Capture updated thread count and safety status after the worker thread completes.
8. **Return Status**: Returns a `ThreadSafetyStatus` struct containing all required metrics.</answer>

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {

    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount.get();
    
    batch.close();
    
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    return {closedInitially, closedAfterClose, recomputeCountAfterExecute};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <unordered_set>

namespace reaction {
    using NodeSet = std::unordered_set<std::shared_ptr<ObserverNode>>;
}

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        reaction::ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Register nodes with the observer graph
    graph.addNode(nodeA);
    graph.addNode(nodeB);
    graph.addNode(nodeC);

    // Set up direct observer relationship: nodeA observes nodeB
    graph.addObserver(nodeB, nodeA);

    // Set up transitive observer relationship: nodeB observes nodeC
    graph.addObserver(nodeC, nodeB);

    // Collect observers of nodeC (should include nodeB and nodeA)
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.getObservers(nodeC, observers);

    // Populate cache data by triggering value changes
    nodeB->valueChanged();
    nodeC->valueChanged();

    // Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = graph.getCacheStats();

    // Trigger cache cleanup
    graph.cleanupCache();

    // Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = graph.getCacheStats();

    // Close all nodes
    graph.closeNode(nodeA);
    graph.closeNode(nodeB);
    graph.closeNode(nodeC);

    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        statsBefore.graphEntries,
        statsAfter.graphEntries,
        statsAfter.graphHitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyTransition {
    bool beforeEnable;
    bool afterEnable;
    bool afterReset;
    std::size_t threadCount;
};

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    auto& tm = reaction::concurrency::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Reset thread manager state
    tm.reset();
    
    // Register current thread
    tm.registerCurrentThread();
    
    // Get thread count after registration
    std::size_t threadCount = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    // Get thread safety status before enabling
    bool beforeEnable = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Manually enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Get thread safety status after enabling
    bool afterEnable = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Reset thread manager state again
    tm.reset();
    
    // Get thread safety status after reset
    bool afterReset = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Return the transition
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = beforeEnable,
        .afterEnable = afterEnable,
        .afterReset = afterReset,
        .threadCount = threadCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    // Get the thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Reset the thread manager state
    threadManager.reset();
    
    // Get thread safety status and count before guard
    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // Create worker thread with ThreadRegistrationGuard
    std::thread worker([&]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard guard;
        // Simulate some work
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));
    });
    
    // Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // Get thread safety status and count after guard
    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    return ThreadGuardState{
        safetyBeforeGuard,
        safetyAfterGuard,
        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : value_(value) {}
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() { return value_; }
private:
    reaction::Var<int> value_;
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead = first.getId(); // Verify stability
    
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();
    
    // Update reactive fields to avoid unused variable warnings
    first.valueField() = 42;
    second.valueField() = 100;
    
    return {firstIdFirstRead, firstIdSecondRead, secondId};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

1. **Class Definition**: `IdTrackedFields` inherits from `reaction::FieldBase` and contains a reactive integer variable `value_`.
2. **Constructor**: Initializes the reactive variable with the provided value.
3. **valueField()**: Returns a reference to the reactive variable for updating.

#pragma once

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Create report struct
    StackMonitorReport report;
    
    // Step 4: Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // Step 5: Create StackGuard in a scope block
    {
        auto guard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::createGuard();
        // Step 6: Check if guard was successfully allocated
        report.guardAllocated = guard.wasAllocated();
        
        if (report.guardAllocated) {
            // Step 7: Get current usage during guard
            report.usageDuringGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
            
            // Step 8: Get statistics
            auto stats = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStatistics();
            
            // Step 9: Calculate usage percentage and check near limit
            report.usagePercentage = (static_cast<double>(report.usageDuringGuard) / stats.totalStackSize) * 100.0;
            report.nearLimitDuringGuard = report.usagePercentage >= 80.0; // Assuming 80% threshold
        }
    } // Guard automatically released when scope exits
    
    // Step 11: Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
    
    // Step 12: Disable monitoring again
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 13: Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // Step 14: Return the report
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Lambda to create large payload
    auto make_large_payload = [largeSeed](int seed) -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = seed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create small resource
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> small_resource(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    
    // Create large resource
    SBOResource<LargePayload> large_resource(make_large_payload(largeSeed));

    // Update small resource and check if change was reported
    bool changed = small_resource.update_value(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});
    bool updateReportedChange = changed;

    // Get storage information
    auto small_info = small_resource.get_storage_info();
    auto large_info = large_resource.get_storage_info();

    SboStorageStatus status{
        updateReportedChange,
        small_info.is_using_sbo(),
        small_info.stack_usage(),
        large_info.is_initialized(),
        large_info.is_using_sbo()
    };

    return status;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**
1. **Lambda Function:** `make_large_payload` generates a `LargePayload` with sequential values starting from `largeSeed`.
2. **SBOResource Creation:** 
   - `small_resource` is initialized with `SmallPayload{initialSmall}`.
   - `large_resource` uses the lambda to generate its payload.
3. **Update Operation:** The small resource's value is updated, and whether the change was reported is tracked via `update_value()`'s return value.
4. **Storage Information:** Both resources' storage details (SBO usage, stack usage, initialization status) are retrieved using `get_storage_info()`.
5. **Status Population:** The `SboStorageStatus` struct is populated with the collected information.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConditionalMutexResult {
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety;
    bool tryLockWithSafety;
};

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    auto& tm = thread_manager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    
    ConditionalMutex mutex;
    
    // Create ConditionalLockGuard without safety enabled
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety = false;
    {
        ConditionalLockGuard guard(mutex, false);
        tryLockWithoutSafety = true; // Guard successfully locked the mutex
    }
    
    // Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Create ConditionalLockGuard with safety enabled  
    bool tryLockWithSafety = false;
    {
        ConditionalLockGuard guard(mutex, true);
        tryLockWithSafety = true; // Guard successfully locked the mutex
    }
    
    return {tryLockWithoutSafety, tryLockWithSafety};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Thread Manager Setup**: The function starts by obtaining the singleton thread manager instance and resetting its state to ensure clean testing conditions.

2. **ConditionalMutex Creation**: A `ConditionalMutex` instance is created to test the conditional locking behavior.

3. **First Lock Attempt (Without Safety)**: 
   - A `ConditionalLockGuard` is created with safety disabled (`false`).
   - The guard automatically attempts to lock the mutex upon construction.
   - The `tryLockWithoutSafety` flag is set to `true` since the guard successfully acquires the lock (assuming the mutex was available).

4. **Enable Thread Safety**: The thread safety feature is enabled via the thread manager, simulating a change in system configuration.

5. **Second Lock Attempt (With Safety)**:
   - Another `ConditionalLockGuard` is created with safety enabled (`true`).
   - The guard attempts to lock the mutex, and `tryLockWithSafety` is set to `true` if successful.

6. **Automatic Cleanup**: Both guards automatically unlock the mutex when they go out of scope, ensuring proper resource management.

This solution follows RAII principles and demonstrates the conditional locking behavior with and without thread safety enabled.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    auto& threadManager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;

    // Step 4: Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    bool sharedAcquired1 = mutex.try_shared_lock();
    
    // Step 5: Unlock if acquired
    if (sharedAcquired1) {
        mutex.unlock_shared();
    }

    {
        // Step 6: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard
        ConditionalSharedLockGuard guard(mutex);
        
        // Step 7: Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared is held without safety
        bool uniqueAcquired1 = mutex.try_unique_lock();
        
        // Step 8: Unlock if acquired
        if (uniqueAcquired1) {
            mutex.unlock_unique();
        }
    } // Guard scope ends here

    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enable_safety();

    // Step 11: Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    bool sharedAcquired2 = mutex.try_shared_lock();
    
    // Step 12: Unlock if acquired
    if (sharedAcquired2) {
        mutex.unlock_shared();
    }

    {
        // Step 13: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard again
        ConditionalSharedLockGuard guard(mutex);
        
        // Step 14: Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared is held with safety
        bool uniqueAcquired2 = mutex.try_unique_lock();
        
        // Step 15: Unlock if acquired
        if (uniqueAcquired2) {
            mutex.unlock_unique();
        }
    } // Second guard scope ends here

    // Step 17: Return the result
    return SharedMutexResult{
        .trySharedWithoutSafety = sharedAcquired1,
        .tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = uniqueAcquired1,
        .trySharedWithSafety = sharedAcquired2,
        .tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = uniqueAcquired2
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**
- **Thread Manager:** The `ThreadManager::getInstance()` retrieves the singleton instance, and `reset()` clears its state.
- **ConditionalSharedMutex:** This mutex supports both shared and unique locking with conditional safety features.
- **RAII Guards:** `ConditionalSharedLockGuard` ensures the shared lock is automatically released when it goes out of scope.
- **Safety Modes:** The `enable_safety()` call activates additional safety checks, though the core locking behavior remains consistent.
- **Result Tracking:** Each lock attempt's success is recorded in the `SharedMutexResult` struct, reflecting the expected behavior where unique locks cannot be acquired while a shared lock is held.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct TinyPod {
    int a;
    int b;
};

struct MediumPod {
    double values[3];
};

struct LargePod {
    std::byte payload[128];
};

struct SboTraitSummary {
    bool tinyAlwaysSbo;
    bool tinyForceHeap;
    bool mediumConditionalSbo;
    bool mediumForceHeap;
    bool largeForceHeap;
    std::size_t alwaysThreshold;
    std::size_t conditionalThreshold;
    std::size_t maxStackUsage;
};

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;
    
    // Get SBO traits for TinyPod
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = reaction::SboTraits<TinyPod>::always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = reaction::SboTraits<TinyPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for MediumPod
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = reaction::SboTraits<MediumPod>::conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = reaction::SboTraits<MediumPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for LargePod
    summary.largeForceHeap = reaction::SboTraits<LargePod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO configuration thresholds
    auto thresholds = reaction::MemoryConfig::get_sbo_thresholds();
    summary.alwaysThreshold = thresholds.always_threshold;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = thresholds.conditional_threshold;
    summary.maxStackUsage = thresholds.max_stack_usage;
    
    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This solution assumes the existence of `reaction::SboTraits` and `reaction::MemoryConfig` with the required methods. The actual implementation would depend on the specific API provided by the `reaction` library.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    
    reaction::ReactionException ex(message, code);
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = ex.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = ex.what();
    info.file = file;
    info.line = line;
    info.function = function;
    info.codeName = ex.getErrorCodeName();
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode, public reaction::FieldNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        if (changed) {
            m_notifications++;
        }
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }
    
private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Create owner and field nodes
    auto ownerNode = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto fieldNode = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");
    
    // Register nodes in observer graph
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(ownerNode);
    observerGraph.registerNode(fieldNode);
    
    // Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register field nodes under object ID
    std::vector<reaction::FieldNode*> fieldNodes = {fieldNode.get()};
    fieldGraph.addObj(objectId, fieldNodes);
    
    // Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bind(objectId, fieldNode, ownerNode);
    
    // Trigger notification on field
    fieldNode->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = ownerNode->notifications() > 0;
    
    // Clear owner notification count
    ownerNode->clear();
    
    // Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearDependencies(ownerNode, fieldNode);
    
    // Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.removeObj(objectId);
    
    // Attempt to bind field again
    bool bindAgainResult = fieldGraph.bind(objectId, fieldNode, ownerNode);
    
    // Trigger notification on field again
    fieldNode->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = ownerNode->notifications() > 0;
    
    // Close nodes
    observerGraph.closeNode(ownerNode);
    observerGraph.closeNode(fieldNode);
    
    return {notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **TrackingFieldNode Class**: Inherits from both `ObserverNode` and `FieldNode` to support field graph operations. Implements notification tracking with `valueChanged()` and `notifications()` methods.

2. **Field Graph Cleanup Process**:
   - **Node Creation & Registration**: Owner and field nodes are created and registered with the observer graph.
   - **Field Binding**: The field is bound to the owner under the specified object ID.
   - **Notification Testing**: A notification triggers on the field, and the owner's notification count is checked.
   - **Cleanup Steps**: Dependencies are cleared, field mappings are removed, and the field is unbound.
   - **Post-Cleanup Verification**: Attempts to re-bind and trigger notifications to confirm the owner no longer receives updates.

3. **Return Result**: The function returns a `FieldGraphCleanupResult` indicating whether notifications occurred before and after cleanup, verifying proper field graph behavior.</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<void>>();
    
    // Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool usesSboStorage = resource->isUsingSbo();
    
    // Get value and check if it's Void type
    auto value = resource->getValue();
    bool retrievedIsVoid = std::is_same_v<decltype(value), reaction::Void>;
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register resource node with the given name
    graph.registerNode(resource, nodeName);
    
    // Verify the name was assigned correctly
    bool nameAssigned = false;
    if (auto node = graph.getNode(resource)) {
        nameAssigned = (node->getName() == nodeName);
    }
    
    // Close the node
    graph.closeNode(resource);
    
    // Check if name was cleared after closing
    bool nameClearedAfterClose = true;
    if (auto node = graph.getNode(resource)) {
        nameClearedAfterClose = false; // Node still exists, name wasn't cleared
    }
    
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = retrievedIsVoid,
        .usesSboStorage = usesSboStorage,
        .nameAssigned = nameAssigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = nameClearedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

5. **State Validation**:

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    auto firstSource = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto secondSource = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto thirdSource = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // Register all nodes with the observer graph
    graph.registerNode(firstSource);
    graph.registerNode(secondSource);
    graph.registerNode(thirdSource);
    
    auto observer = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    
    // Step 5: Attach the observer to the first source
    graph.addObserver(observer, firstSource);
    
    // Step 6: Trigger notification on the first source
    firstSource->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Step 7: Get notification count after initial setup
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial = observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 8: Rewire dependencies - detach from first source and attach to second
    graph.removeObserver(observer, firstSource);
    graph.addObserver(observer, secondSource);
    
    // Step 9: Trigger notification on the old (first) source
    firstSource->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Step 10: Get notification count after old source notification
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 11: Trigger notification on the second source
    secondSource->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Step 12: Get notification count after second source notification
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 13: Trigger notification on the third source
    thirdSource->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Step 14: Get notification count after third source notification
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 15: Close all nodes
    firstSource->close();
    secondSource->close();
    thirdSource->close();
    observer->close();
    
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstNotificationsAfterInitial,
        firstNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        secondNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Node Creation & Registration**: Three `ObserverNode` instances and a `CountingObserver` are created and registered with the `ObserverGraph`.

2. **Observer Attachment**: The counting observer is initially attached to the first source node.

3. **Notification Flow**: 
   - First source triggers the observer (count = 1)
   - After rewiring (detaching from first, attaching to second), old source notifications don't affect the observer (count remains 1)
   - Second source triggers the observer (count = 2)
   - Third source triggers the observer (count = 3)

4. **Cleanup**: All nodes are properly closed before returning the snapshot.

The solution correctly implements the reactive notification flow and dependency management as described in the problem.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    REACTION_REGISTER_VAR(0);
    static int calcEvals = 0;
    REACTION_REGISTER_CALC([&]() { return var; }, &calcEvals);

    // Apply atomic addition
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(var, 5);
    int afterAddition = var.get();

    // Apply atomic multiplication
    reaction::atomicMulAssign(var, 2);
    int afterMultiplication = var.get();

    // Apply atomic bitwise AND
    reaction::atomicAndAssign(var, 0xF);
    int afterBitwiseAnd = var.get();

    // Apply atomic left shift
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(var, 2);
    int afterShift = var.get();

    // Apply post-increment
    int postIncrementReturn = reaction::atomicPostIncrement(var);
    int finalValue = var.get();

    AtomicCompoundSnapshot snapshot{
        afterAddition,
        afterMultiplication,
        afterBitwiseAnd,
        afterShift,
        postIncrementReturn,
        finalValue,
        calcEvals
    };

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate ObserverNode::updateDepth cooperating with BatchCompare ordering.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot Comparator outcomes after adjusting node depths.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    // Step 1: Define DepthProbeNode class
    struct DepthProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
        void updateDepth(int depth) override {
            m_depth.store(depth);
        }
    };

    // Step 2: Get thread manager instance
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();

    // Step 5: Create shallow and deep DepthProbeNode instances
    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();

    // Step 6: Create NodeWeak from nodes
    auto shallow_weak = std::weak_ptr<ObserverNode>(shallow);
    auto deep_weak = std::weak_ptr<ObserverNode>(deep);

    // Step 7: Create BatchCompare instance with custom comparator
    auto batch_compare = std::make_shared<reaction::BatchCompare>(
        std::vector<std::weak_ptr<ObserverNode>>{shallow_weak, deep_weak},
        [](const auto& lhs, const auto& rhs) {
            auto left = lhs.lock();
            auto right = rhs.lock();
            // Handle expired weak pointers by treating them as invalid
            if (!left || !right) return false;
            return left->m_depth.load() < right->m_depth.load();
        }
    );

    // Step 8: Compare initial nodes (depth 0)
    bool initialComparatorResult = batch_compare->compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);

    // Step 9: Update depths
    shallow->updateDepth(1);
    deep->updateDepth(2);

    // Step 10: Compare shallow before deep
    bool shallowBeforeDeep = batch_compare->compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);

    // Step 11: Update shallow depth again
    shallow->updateDepth(3);

    // Step 12: Compare deep before shallow
    bool deepBeforeShallow = batch_compare->compare(deep_weak, shallow_weak);

    // Step 13: Create null NodeWeak
    auto null_weak = std::weak_ptr<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 14: Compare null with shallow
    bool nullComesFirst = batch_compare->compare(null_weak, shallow_weak);

    // Step 15: Return depth ordering snapshot
    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        initialComparatorResult,
        shallowBeforeDeep,
        deepBeforeShallow,
        nullComesFirst
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**
1. **DepthProbeNode**: Inherits from `reaction::ObserverNode` and overrides `updateDepth` to store the depth value.
2. **Thread Manager**: Resets and enables thread safety for consistent behavior.
3. **Node Creation**: Two nodes (`shallow` and `deep`) are created with initial depth 0.
4. **Weak Pointers**: Convert shared pointers to weak pointers for safe comparison.
5. **BatchCompare**: Compares nodes using a custom comparator that checks node validity and compares depths.
6. **Depth Updates**: Nodes' depths are adjusted to demonstrate ordering changes.
7. **Null Handling**: Compares a null weak pointer to demonstrate null precedence.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Create reactive variables for a and b
    auto react_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto react_b = reaction::var(b);

    // Build negated sum expression: -(react_a + react_b)
    auto sum_expr = make_binary_op(AddOp{}, react_a, react_b);
    auto negated_sum_expr = make_unary_op(NegOp{}, sum_expr);
    auto negated_sum_var = reaction::var(negated_sum_expr);

    // Create reactive boolean variable (initialized to true)
    auto bool_var = reaction::var(true);

    // Build logical NOT expression: !bool_var
    auto logical_not_expr = make_unary_op(NotOp{}, bool_var);
    auto logical_not_var = reaction::var(logical_not_expr);

    // Create reactive integer variable (initialized to a)
    auto int_var = reaction::var(a);

    // Build bitwise NOT expression: ~int_var
    auto bitwise_not_expr = make_unary_op(BitwiseNotOp{}, int_var);
    auto bitwise_not_var = reaction::var(bitwise_not_expr);

    // Get initial values before any updates
    int initialNegatedSum = negated_sum_var.get();
    bool initialLogicalNot = logical_not_var.get(); // Initially false (because bool_var is true)
    int initialBitwiseNot = bitwise_not_var.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    // Update react_a and react_b
    react_a.set(a + 1);
    react_b.set(b + 1);

    // Flip the boolean variable
    bool_var.set(!bool_var.get());

    // Update the integer variable
    int_var.set(a + 10);

    // Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = negated_sum_var.get();
    bool updatedLogicalNot = logical_not_var.get();
    int updatedBitwiseNot = bitwise_not_var.get();

    // Return the snapshot
    return {
        initialNegatedSum,
        updatedNegatedSum,
        updatedLogicalNot,
        updatedBitwiseNot
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Reactive Variables Creation**: 
   - `react_a` and `react_b` are created from input parameters `a` and `b`.
   - `bool_var` is a reactive boolean initialized to `true`.
   - `int_var` is a reactive integer initialized to `a`.

2. **Expression Building**:
   - **Negated Sum**: Uses `make_binary_op` for addition and `make_unary_op` for negation.
   - **Logical NOT**: Directly applies `make_unary_op` to `bool_var`.
   - **Bitwise NOT**: Applies `make_unary_op` to `int_var`.

3. **Value Capture**:
   - Initial values are captured before any updates.
   - After updating variables (`react_a`, `react_b`, `bool_var`, `int_var`), new values are retrieved.

4. **Return Structure**: 
   - `initialNegatedSum`: Original sum negation.
   - `updatedNegatedSum`: Sum after updating `react_a` and `react_b`.
   - `logicalNotAfterFlip`: Logical NOT after flipping `bool_var`.
   - `bitwiseNotValue`: Bitwise NOT after updating `int_var`.

This solution demonstrates reactive programming principles where expressions automatically update when their dependencies change.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterCache;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool metricsCachedExists;
    bool metricsClearedAfterInvalidate;
};

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    NodeMetricsCache cache;
    auto node = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Get cache statistics before caching
    auto stats_before = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = stats_before.entries_count;

    // Cache node metrics
    cache.cacheNodeMetrics(node);

    // Get cache statistics after caching
    auto stats_after_cache = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterCache = stats_after_cache.entries_count;
    std::size_t hitCount = stats_after_cache.hit_count;
    std::size_t missCount = stats_after_cache.miss_count;
    double hitRatio = static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount);

    // Invalidate all entries
    cache.invalidateAll();

    // Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto stats_after_invalidate = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = stats_after_invalidate.entries_count;

    // Determine boolean flags
    bool metricsCachedExists = entriesAfterCache > entriesBefore;
    bool metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate == 0;

    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterCache,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,
        hitCount,
        missCount,
        hitRatio,
        metricsCachedExists,
        metricsClearedAfterInvalidate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    using namespace std;
    using namespace reaction;
    
    // Define IntCache class inheriting from CacheBase
    class IntCache : public CacheBase<int, int> {
    public:
        using CacheBase<int, int>::CacheBase;
    };
    
    // Create IntCache instance
    IntCache<int> cache;
    
    // Get initial cache statistics
    auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.entries;
    std::size_t initialHitCount = initialStats.hitCount;
    std::size_t initialMissCount = initialStats.missCount;
    
    // Perform initial lookup (should be a miss)
    auto result1 = cache.get(1);
    bool firstLookupWasMiss = (result1 == nullptr);
    
    // Insert value into cache
    cache.put(1, 42);
    
    // Perform second lookup (should be a hit)
    auto result2 = cache.get(1);
    bool secondLookupWasHit = (result2 != nullptr);
    
    // Get cache statistics after insertion
    auto afterInsertStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = afterInsertStats.entries;
    std::size_t hitCount = afterInsertStats.hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount = afterInsertStats.missCount;
    
    // Calculate hit ratio
    double hitRatio = 0.0;
    if (hitCount + missCount > 0) {
        hitRatio = static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount);
    }
    
    // Invalidate all cache entries
    cache.invalidateAll();
    
    // Trigger cleanup
    cache.cleanupOldEntries();
    
    // Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto finalStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = finalStats.entries;
    
    // Return generic cache stats snapshot
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterInsert,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,
        hitCount,
        missCount,
        hitRatio,
        firstLookupWasMiss,
        secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GlobalStateSnapshot {
    bool trackingActiveBefore;
    bool batchActiveBefore;
    bool batchFunActiveBefore;

    bool trackingActiveInRegGuard;
    bool batchFunActiveInBatchGuard;
    bool batchActiveInBatchExeGuard;

    bool trackingActiveAfter;
    bool batchActiveAfter;
    bool batchFunActiveAfter;
};

GlobalStateSnapshot test_global_state_scoped_guards() {
    reaction::concurrency::resetGlobalState();
    
    // Check state before any guards
    auto state = reaction::concurrency::getGlobalState();
    GlobalStateSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.trackingActiveBefore = state.isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveBefore = state.isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveBefore = state.isBatchFunActive();

    // RegFunGuard scope
    {
        reaction::concurrency::RegFunGuard g;
        state = reaction::concurrency::getGlobalState();
        snapshot.trackingActiveInRegGuard = state.isTrackingActive();
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = state.isBatchFunActive();
    }

    // BatchFunGuard scope
    {
        reaction::concurrency::BatchFunGuard g;
        state = reaction::concurrency::getGlobalState();
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = state.isBatchFunActive();
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = state.isBatchActive();
    }

    // BatchExeGuard scope
    {
        reaction::concurrency::BatchExeGuard g;
        state = reaction::concurrency::getGlobalState();
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = state.isBatchActive();
    }

    // Reset global state after guards
    reaction::concurrency::resetGlobalState();
    
    // Check state after guards
    state = reaction::concurrency::getGlobalState();
    snapshot.trackingActiveAfter = state.isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveAfter = state.isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveAfter = state.isBatchFunActive();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <atomic>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto &manager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    manager.reset();  // Reset thread manager state
    
    // Check thread safety before any registration
    bool safetyBeforeForce = manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Register main thread
    manager.registerThread();
    
    // Check thread safety after main thread registration
    bool safetyAfterMainForce = manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Create and register worker thread
    std::thread worker([&manager]() {
        manager.registerThread();
    });
    
    // Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // Check thread safety after worker thread registration
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce = manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{
        safetyBeforeForce,
        safetyAfterMainForce,
        safetyAfterWorkerForce
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Thread Manager Instance**: `ThreadManager::getInstance()` retrieves the singleton instance.
2. **Reset State**: `manager.reset()` clears any previous registrations and resets internal state.
3. **Pre-registration Check**: `isThreadSafetyEnabled()` returns `false` since no threads are registered yet.
4. **Main Thread Registration**: `registerThread()` registers the current (main) thread.
5. **Post-main Check**: Thread safety remains disabled because only one thread exists.
6. **Worker Thread**: Created with a lambda that registers the worker thread.
7. **Thread Synchronization**: `join()` ensures the worker thread completes before proceeding.
8. **Post-worker Check**: Thread safety becomes enabled due to multiple thread registration.
9. **Return Snapshot**: All three safety states are captured in the returned struct.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto A = var(a);
    auto B = var(b);
    auto C = var(c);

    // Build sum expression: A + B
    auto sum_expr = make_binary_expr(ADD_OP, A, B);

    // Build negated expression: -(A + B)
    auto negated_expr = make_unary_expr(NEG_OP, sum_expr);

    // Build chained expression: -(A + B) * C
    auto chained_expr = make_binary_expr(MUL_OP, negated_expr, C);

    // Get initial values
    int initialSum = get(sum_expr);
    int initialNegatedSum = get(negated_expr);
    int initialChainedValue = get(chained_expr);

    // Update reactive variables to trigger recomputation
    set(A, a + 10);  // Update A to a new value
    set(C, c + 5);   // Update C to a new value

    // Get updated values after recomputation
    int updatedSum = get(sum_expr);
    int updatedNegatedSum = get(negated_expr);
    int updatedChainedValue = get(chained_expr);

    return RawExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialSum = initialSum,
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .initialChainedValue = initialChainedValue,
        .updatedSum = updatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .updatedChainedValue = updatedChainedValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Reactive Variables**: `var(a)`, `var(b)`, and `var(c)` create reactive variables initialized with the input values.

2. **Expression Building**:
   - `sum_expr`: Uses `make_binary_expr` with `ADD_OP` to create `A + B`.
   - `negated_expr`: Wraps `sum_expr` in `make_unary_expr` with `NEG_OP` to negate the sum.
   - `chained_expr`: Combines `negated_expr` and `C` with `make_binary_expr` and `MUL_OP`.

3. **Value Capture**: The `get()` function retrieves current expression values before and after updates.

4. **Recomputation Trigger**: `set()` updates reactive variables, causing dependent expressions to automatically recompute.

5. **Result Snapshot**: Returns a struct containing both initial and updated expression values, demonstrating reactive computation behavior.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) 
        : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}
    
    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op) {
        m_lastReportedChange = op(m_value);
    }
    
    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op, bool forceNotify) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = changed || forceNotify;
    }
    
    int32_t value() const { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    int32_t m_value;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl impl(initial);
    
    // Apply atomic add assignment
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(impl, 5);
    bool addReported = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Apply atomic multiply assignment
    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(impl, 2);
    bool multiplyReported = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Apply atomic bitwise AND assignment
    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(impl, 0xFFFF);
    bool andReported = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Apply atomic left shift assignment
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(impl, 2);
    bool leftShiftReported = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Apply atomic increment
    reaction::atomicIncrement(impl);
    bool incrementReported = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    int32_t finalValue = impl.value();
    
    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .addReportedChange = addReported,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyReported,
        .andReportedChange = andReported,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftReported,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementReported
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
